seed_problem: Select a maximum-weight set of mutually non-conflicting research sites on a conflict graph; checker validates independence and scores achieved weight against a single-best baseline.
mutation: input: generalize the conflict graph from an easy structured class (bipartite/interval) to an arbitrary dense general graph with per-target values, so maximum-weight independent set selection becomes NP-hard and weight-greedy vs. weight/degree vs. randomized local-search strategies diverge.
title: Aperture Atlas: Conflict-Free Sky Docket
strategies: single-best baseline: schedule only the highest-value target (always feasible, ratio 0.1)
weight-greedy: sort targets by value descending, add each if it conflicts with nothing already scheduled
weight-over-degree greedy: prioritize by value/(1+remaining conflict degree), removing neighbors as targets are locked in
randomized multi-restart with (1,2)-swap local search: perturb the greedy order, build a maximal docket, then improve via remove-one/add-more swaps and keep the best docket found
